title: Best VPN for New Zealand 2026: NordVPN, ExpressVPN and Mullvad Compared
description: The best VPNs for New Zealand in 2026, tested for TVNZ unblocking, Five Eyes privacy, and speed across Pacific routes. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, Mullvad and Proton VPN compared.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a VPN in New Zealand?

acceptedAnswer.text: New Zealand has relatively liberal internet laws compared to Five Eyes partners, but it is a full member of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance, which means your browsing data can be shared with US, UK, Canadian, and Australian agencies. ISPs are not legally required to retain browsing logs, but VPN use in New Zealand is common among privacy-conscious users, remote workers accessing company networks, and anyone wanting to bypass region restrictions on streaming content from the US, UK, or Australia.

mainEntity.name: Which VPN has the best servers in New Zealand?

acceptedAnswer.text: NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark all maintain physical servers in New Zealand (Auckland). NordVPN has the largest server count in the country. For users outside New Zealand trying to get a New Zealand IP address (for content like TVNZ or Three Now), ExpressVPN consistently unblocks New Zealand streaming platforms. For users inside New Zealand who want good speeds to nearby regions (Australia, Japan, Singapore), Mullvad VPN delivers low latency due to efficient routing infrastructure in the Pacific.

mainEntity.name: Is TVNZ blocked outside New Zealand?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. TVNZ (tvnz.co.nz) and Three Now (threenow.co.nz) are geo-restricted to New Zealand IP addresses. To access TVNZ outside New Zealand, you need a VPN with a New Zealand server. ExpressVPN and NordVPN reliably unblock TVNZ in 2026 testing. Some free VPNs and smaller providers are blocked by TVNZ's geoblocking system. The streaming platform checks both IP address and sometimes DNS origin, so use a provider that includes DNS leak protection.

content:
New Zealand has some of the best internet infrastructure in the Southern Hemisphere and relatively open internet laws, but VPN use is growing there for three distinct reasons: Five Eyes privacy concerns, streaming access for New Zealanders traveling abroad (or expats wanting TVNZ), and remote workers accessing corporate networks. Here are the best options in 2026.

Best VPNs for New Zealand in 2026

1. NordVPN

Best overall for New Zealand. NordVPN maintains a solid server presence in Auckland and routes traffic efficiently across the Pacific. Speeds to Australia, Japan, and Singapore are strong, which matters for New Zealand users whose nearest international hubs are in those directions. NordVPN consistently unblocks TVNZ, Three Now, and Australian streaming services. Price: from $3.39 per month on a 2-year plan.

2. ExpressVPN

Best for streaming. ExpressVPN has a long track record of unblocking georestricted content from New Zealand, Australia, the US, and UK. It reliably bypasses TVNZ geoblocking and is the most consistent option tested across multiple New Zealand streaming platforms. More expensive than competitors at $8.32 per month, but worth it if streaming is the primary use case.

3. Surfshark

Best value. Surfshark allows unlimited simultaneous connections, which makes it the most cost-effective option for families or users with multiple devices. New Zealand server performance is solid though not exceptional. Unblocks major streaming services reliably. From $2.49 per month on a 2-year plan.

4. Mullvad

Best for privacy. Mullvad has no accounts, accepts cash payment, and publishes regular transparency reports. For New Zealand users primarily concerned about Five Eyes surveillance rather than streaming access, Mullvad is the most privacy-focused mainstream option. Fixed price of $5 per month with no long-term contracts or upsells.

5. Proton VPN

Best free option with upgrade path. Proton VPN offers a genuinely usable free tier (no data cap, three server locations) and is based in Switzerland, outside Five Eyes jurisdiction. The free tier does not include New Zealand servers, but the paid plan does. From $4.99 per month paid annually.

VPN Laws in New Zealand

VPN use is completely legal in New Zealand. There is no government registry or licensing requirement for VPN users. New Zealand ISPs are not required to retain customer browsing data under current law, but New Zealand participates in the Five Eyes signals intelligence sharing arrangement with the US, UK, Canada, and Australia. This means that using a VPN to avoid ISP-level tracking has value for users concerned about surveillance, though most everyday New Zealand internet users are not actively monitored.

Accessing TVNZ and Three Now Outside New Zealand

TVNZ and Three Now require a New Zealand IP address. Both platforms actively block VPN IP ranges, which means some providers that worked in 2024 may be blocked in 2026. As of mid-2026, ExpressVPN and NordVPN reliably unblock both services. Surfshark works for TVNZ but has occasional blocks on Three Now.

If you are a New Zealand expat living in Australia, the UK, or the US and want to keep watching New Zealand content, run a speed test from each VPN before subscribing to confirm their New Zealand servers are not blocked by the platform you need.

Speed Expectations in New Zealand

New Zealand's international internet connectivity routes through Australia (Sydney, primarily) before reaching the US and Asia. VPN servers in Sydney or Auckland will give the best performance for New Zealand users. Servers in Europe or the eastern US add 250 to 350ms of latency, which is manageable for browsing but affects real-time applications. For gaming and video calls, stick to VPN servers in Australia, Japan, or Singapore for the lowest possible latency.
